reasoning: Capillariasis is a rare parasitic disease caused by nematode worms. It is easily treated with anti-parasitic drugs like mebendazole or albendazole. There are no vaccines for human nematode infections, and creating one is extremely difficult scientifically. Even if there were a breakthrough, developing, approving, and rolling out a vaccine takes years, not weeks or months. Furthermore, there is no economic or public health incentive to fast-track a vaccine for such a rare condition. Given that the resolution dates are all within 3 months of the knowledge cutoff, there is virtually zero chance that a vaccine will be created, approved, and added to the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page by any of the forecast dates. I am setting the probabilities at the absolute minimum allowed value of 0.02 for all three horizons.
